key_insight: BAs3 and BP3 monolayers are predicted by DFT to be stable, metallic, and exceptionally promising sodium-ion battery anodes with very high capacity and fast Na diffusion.
engineering_relevance: These 2D materials could enable higher-energy-density, fast-charging sodium-ion batteries if their predicted low voltage, high conductivity, and rapid ion transport translate into practical electrodes.
method: First-principles DFT screening of BX3 (X = As, P) monolayers
Na adsorption-site and thermodynamic affinity analysis
Na-ion migration barrier, PDOS, and average open-circuit voltage evaluation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports ultralow Na migration barriers of 0.19 eV (BAs3) and 0.26 eV (BP3), along with predicted capacities of 1365 and 3875 mAh g-1, respectively.
watch_item: The results are computational predictions only, so experimental synthesis stability, cycling durability, and electrode integration still need validation.
paper_title: Exploring the Potential of BY3 (Y = P, As) Monolayers as High-Capacity and Rapid-Diffusion Anodes for Sodium-Ion Batteries
